name: test-coverage
description: Raise coverage that means something: find risky untested surface, write tests, then sabotage the code to prove each test actually bites. Tests that survive sabotage of their own subject get deleted, not shipped.

nodes.label: Risk survey
nodes.promptContext: Repurposed as a coverage strategist: run the project's coverage tooling, rank untested surface by blast radius (public API > branching logic > error paths > glue), and spec the top 5 targets as concrete test descriptions. Coverage percentage is context, not the goal.

nodes.label: Write tests
nodes.promptContext: Work COVERAGE_PLAN.md top-down. The sabotage step in your method is mandatory here — record each mutation and the failing output it produced.

nodes.label: Audit tests
nodes.promptContext: Attack the new tests, not the code: are assertions specific or vacuous? Would they survive a revert of the code they claim to guard (they must NOT)? Any test testing the mock instead of the subject?

checklist: Every new test has a recorded sabotage failure
Real findings surfaced, not papered over
